title: gastrolobium papilio
intro: Gastrolobium papilio, commonly known as the 'Bird Dangler', is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents present in Gastrolobium papilio include flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic actions like antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. parts: Gastrolobium papilio has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and seeds, which have been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine. The leaves of the Gastrolobium papilio plant are said to contain a number of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant contain compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides, which may have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. side_effects: Gastrolobium papilio can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting, due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can lead to abdominal pain, diarrhea, and potentially life-threatening kidney damage. The plant's toxic glycosides can also cause cardiac problems, including irregular heartbeat and reduced blood pressure.
